2605.03447.title: An ERP Study of Recursive Possessive Parsing in ASD Children and Its Cognitive Neuro Mechanisms
2605.03447.paper_summary: Recursive structures are a core property of human language, yet little is known about how children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) process complex recursion. This ERP study investigated the online processing of two-level recursive possessive structures in Mandarin-speaking children with ASD (n = 12) compared to typically developing (TD) peers (n = 12) using a sentence-picture matching paradigm. ERPs were analyzed for P200 (150-250 ms), N400 (300-500 ms), and P600 (500-1000 ms). Results showed that ASD children exhibited significantly reduced P200 amplitudes and failed to show the typical posterior grammaticality effect, indicating atypical early perceptual processing. No robust N400 violation effect was observed in either group, confirming the mismatch was not a semantic anomaly; however, ASD children showed a reversed anterior effect and an attenuated posterior effect. For the P600, ASD children had significantly reduced amplitudes, no posterior grammaticality effect, and a trend toward delayed latency, reflecting a core deficit in syntactic reanalysis. These findings demonstrate that while lexical-semantic processing is relatively preserved in ASD, the online syntactic computation required for recursion is severely impaired, supporting modular dissociation accounts of language in autism.
2605.03447.paper_summary_zh: 遞歸結構是人類語言的一個核心特徵，但對於自閉症譜系障礙（ASD）兒童如何處理複雜遞歸的情況知之甚少。這項ERP研究調查了使用句子-圖片匹配範式的普通話ASD兒童（n = 12）與典型發展（TD）同齡人（n = 12）在兩級遞歸性所有格結構的在線處理。對P200（150-250毫秒）、N400（300-500毫秒）和P600（500-1000毫秒）進行了ERP分析。結果顯示，ASD兒童的P200振幅顯著降低，並未顯示典型的後部語法效應，這表明其早期感知處理異常。兩組中均未觀察到明顯的N400違規效應，確認不匹配並非語義異常；然而，ASD兒童顯示出相反的前部效應和減弱的後部效應。對於P600，ASD兒童的振幅顯著降低，沒有後部語法效應，並且潛伏期延遲的趨勢反映出語法再分析的核心缺陷。這些發現表明，儘管ASD中的詞彙-語義處理相對保留，但進行遞歸所需的在線語法計算受到嚴重損害，支持了自閉症語言模塊解離的觀點。
2605.03447.author: Fu Chenxi et.al.
2605.03447.authors: Fu Chenxi, Wang Xiaoyi, Zhuang Ziman, Yang Caimei
